name: Speed up zhe pre-commit + pre-push hooks

description: Baseline: pre-commit + pre-push end-to-end timing

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline; main hot spot is l2_api (235s).

name: Speed up zhe pre-commit + pre-push hooks (real hook bench)

description: Baseline with real hook bench

asi.hypothesis: Baseline using new bench (real hook scripts) with already-parallel pre-commit/pre-push stages.
asi.bottleneck: pre_push 244s (95% of total) — dominated by L2 API tests (vitest fileParallelism=false, dev server lazy compile).

description: Try fileParallelism=true for L2 (broke tests due to shared TEST_USER)

asi.finding: fileParallelism=true cuts L2 from 234s to 58s (4x), but ~10 tests fail due to shared TEST_USER and global cleanupTestData wiping other files' data. Files using TEST_USER need per-file isolation.
asi.hypothesis: Enable vitest fileParallelism for L2 API tests.
asi.next_action_hint: Migrate seed helpers to use per-file user IDs derived from describe/file path; cleanup scoped to that user only. Files with conflicts: api, backy-pull, tmp-upload, webhook, links, ideas, folders, tags, uploads.
asi.rollback_reason: L2 tests fail under parallel execution due to shared TEST_USER state.

description: Per-file user IDs in 5 root L2 test files; enable fileParallelism (4x)

asi.finding: L2 API tests now run in parallel across files (vitest fileParallelism=true). 5 root tests migrated from shared TEST_USER to per-file user IDs (matching v1/* pattern). pre-push dropped 244s→57s, pre-commit also faster due to better CPU utilization.
asi.hypothesis: Per-file user isolation enables fileParallelism for L2 tests.

description: maxConcurrency 4\u21928 (no effect)

asi.finding: maxConcurrency only affects it.concurrent within a file; no within-file concurrency in our suite. Need different lever.
asi.hypothesis: Bumping vitest maxConcurrency from 4 to 8 will allow more in-flight HTTP.
asi.next_action_hint: Try splitting ideas/links files, or adding it.concurrent within-file. Consider bumping pool threads.

description: Use RETURNING id in seed helpers (one D1 round trip per seed)

asi.finding: RETURNING clause halves D1 round trips per seed call (2→1). Saves ~4s overall.
asi.hypothesis: Use RETURNING id in seedLink/seedUpload/seedIdea to avoid extra SELECT round-trip.

description: Bump maxWorkers to 16 for L2 (all files in flight)

asi.finding: Wall-clock now dominated by single longest file (ideas 49s).
asi.hypothesis: Bumping maxWorkers from default to 16 lets all 15 files run concurrently.

description: Use describe.concurrent on ideas POST/DELETE/Authentication (catastrophic slowdown)

asi.finding: describe.concurrent in ideas test caused 16x slowdown — likely D1 HTTP rate-limiting or contention on shared TEST_USER row. Avoid within-file concurrency until users are per-test.
asi.hypothesis: describe.concurrent on POST/DELETE/Authentication blocks parallelizes within the slowest file.
asi.next_action_hint: Try splitting the file into two physical .test.ts files (or per-method user IDs).

description: Revert maxWorkers:16; keep default (more stable, slightly faster)

asi.finding: maxWorkers:16 caused intermittent ~14min D1 timeouts (overload). Reverting to default workers is stable and slightly faster.
asi.hypothesis: Drop maxWorkers:16 — caused contention/timeouts; default vitest worker count is plenty for 15 files.

description: Parallelize cleanupTestData DELETEs with Promise.all (race condition)

asi.finding: Concurrent DELETEs against D1 don't reliably settle before next call — GET tests after cleanup found stale rows. Likely D1 eventual consistency between parallel commits or HTTP queueing.
asi.hypothesis: Run cleanupTestData DELETEs concurrently via Promise.all to cut wall time.
asi.next_action_hint: Need atomic D1 batch endpoint for cleanup. Try `/d1/database/{id}/query` with array body (D1 batch API).
asi.rollback_reason: Race causes leftover data → test failures.

description: Multi-statement SQL for cleanupTestData (1 round trip, was 9)

asi.finding: D1 multi-statement SQL works without params — 9 DELETEs in one round trip. Saves ~6s per file but only beforeAll/afterAll, hits longest file.
asi.hypothesis: Inline user id and use multi-statement SQL for cleanupTestData.

description: Split ideas.test.ts into ideas + ideas-by-id (parallel files)

asi.finding: Splitting ideas.test.ts into ideas + ideas-by-id (per-file user IDs) brought ideas's wall time 47s→24s. links now dominates at 41s.
asi.hypothesis: Split ideas.test.ts into two physical files with separate user IDs to reduce single-file bottleneck.

description: Split links.test.ts into links + links-by-id (parallel files)

asi.finding: Splitting links.test.ts into links + links-by-id (per-file user IDs) brought wall time from 41s to ~25s on those files. Total now 35s.
asi.hypothesis: Same split pattern for links as ideas.

description: Promise.all for seedApiKey calls in v1 test beforeAll

asi.finding: Marginal improvement; bottleneck is per-file longest test, not setup. Worth keeping for cleanliness even at noise level.
asi.hypothesis: Parallelize the 2-3 seedApiKey calls in beforeAll across all v1/* test files.

description: Split folders.test.ts into folders + folders-by-id

asi.finding: Folders split saves 7s on that file (20s→13s) but doesn't move total because top-2 (links-by-id, ideas-by-id) remain. Improvement is noise-level on total but cleanup makes future splits possible.
asi.hypothesis: Split folders.test.ts into folders + folders-by-id to relieve the longest single-file bottleneck.

description: Move coverage gate from pre-commit to pre-push (CPU contention)

asi.finding: Coverage during pre-push contends with L2 server CPU; L2 slows by ~3s, offsetting pre-commit gain.
asi.hypothesis: Move coverage gate from pre-commit to pre-push (parallel with L2). Saves coverage cost in pre-commit.
asi.next_action_hint: Try running coverage as a separate sequential step after L2, or only on changed files.
asi.rollback_reason: Net total slightly worse (~36s vs 34s) due to CPU contention with dev server.

description: Confirm post-revert baseline

asi.hypothesis: Re-baseline after revert; current best is post-folders-revert state.
asi.note: Best run previously 34.28s; this run 36.57s within noise. No code change.

description: Split links-by-id PATCH into separate file

asi.finding: links-by-id split: 24s→11s for that file. Wall-clock bound by ideas-by-id now.
asi.hypothesis: Split links-by-id PATCH into its own file (PATCH had 9 slow tests).

description: Split ideas-by-id PATCH into separate file

asi.finding: ideas-by-id split mirrors links-by-id (feccc8e). L2 real time 30s→22s (~-27%). New bottleneck is no longer ideas-by-id.
asi.hypothesis: Split ideas-by-id PATCH into its own file (PATCH had many tag/expiry round trips dominating wall time).

name: L3 Playwright BDD E2E speed + flake reduction

description: Baseline: 113 L3 tests serial, workers=1, dev mode (turbopack)

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for L3 optimization
asi.current_config: fullyParallel: false, workers: 1, dev server with turbopack, serial across all 17 spec files
asi.key_observation: All 113 tests pass cleanly in 144s - no current flakes. Bottleneck: workers=1 + serial execution. Tests share the same E2E_TEST_USER, but most operations are scoped per-test with prefixes.

description: workers=2 — file-level parallelism (15 file-bodies use describe.serial)

asi.finding: workers=2 cuts L3 from 143.8s to 85.1s (-41%) with 0 flakes. All 113 tests pass on a single run.
asi.hypothesis: Increase workers to 2 (from 1) — file-level parallelism using existing describe.serial isolation.

description: workers=3 — try further parallelism

asi.finding: workers=3 (112s) is slower than workers=2 (85s). Single Next.js dev server becomes CPU-bound; turbopack's per-route compile causes contention. Sweet spot is 2.
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=3 — maybe more parallelism extracts further wins.
asi.next_action_hint: Try prod-mode build (next start) to remove dev compile bottleneck, then revisit workers>=3.
asi.rollback_reason: Slower than workers=2 baseline due to dev-server contention.

description: next start (prod mode) instead of next dev --turbopack

asi.finding: next start fails immediately: routesManifest.dataRoutes not iterable. Stale .next from a different config. Switching to prod mode requires bundling next build into bench (~60s) which would offset gains unless cached.
asi.hypothesis: Use next start instead of next dev to avoid per-route turbopack compile cost.
asi.next_action_hint: Add this as deferred: pre-build .next once (warm cache outside hook) then start prod server.
asi.rollback_reason: Webserver fails to start; not a viable single-step swap.

description: Pre-warm dashboard routes in global-setup

asi.finding: Pre-warm 75s (1 failed, 9 didn't run) and re-run 99s (1 failed). Failures are real flakes, NOT caused by prewarm. Wall-time 'gain' was illusory: 9 tests skipped by serial-stop. Worth investigating whether prewarm helps once flakes fixed.
asi.hypothesis: Pre-warm common dashboard routes once before tests start to remove per-route compile latency.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the upload-zone strict mode flake first (resolved to 2 elements - likely from React StrictMode dev double-render or skeleton overlay). Then re-test prewarm.
asi.rollback_reason: Pre-warm did not produce a stable improvement; introduced or exposed flakes that masked real timing.

description: Baseline confirm at workers=2 (post-prewarm-revert)

asi.finding: Re-baseline: 79.93s, 113 passed, 0 flake. Workers=2 stable when no extra changes. Variance in subsequent runs is ~5s (75-85s).
asi.hypothesis: Re-confirm post-revert baseline stays at 85s with no flakes.

description: Cache auth: skip setup if storageState cookie still valid

asi.finding: Auth-cache-skip saves ~5s of auth.setup time but it's already overlapped with dev-server warm-up of /dashboard route. Wall-time unchanged (80s vs 80s baseline). Adds complexity for zero benefit.
asi.hypothesis: Skip auth.setup when cached storageState cookie still valid (>1h).
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable wall-time gain; setup time is hidden behind first-route compile.

description: Fix uploads.spec flakes: .first() on text assertions + wait for delete-button enabled

asi.finding: Flake fix: 113 passed, 0 failed. Wall time 83s (within noise of 80s baseline). Test was unreliable when its prior worker had been on the dashboard route just before — a 2nd upload-zone briefly co-existed.
asi.hypothesis: Fix uploads.spec page-render flake: use .first() on text/state assertions; wait for delete button enabled before click.
asi.fix_summary: 1) line 53/54: getByText().first() to handle dev-mode brief double-mount. 2) cleanup test: wait for delete button enabled before click.

description: Split tags.spec into create + filter (wall time same; reveals upload cross-file race)

asi.finding: Tags split: file unchanged in wall time (~85s, within noise), but exposed underlying flake: uploads.spec.ts cleanup test fails when overview.spec.ts (in parallel worker) seeds uploads mid-flight. The upload-item component for the seeded row shows disabled delete button — timing race or stale React state. Need uploads.spec to use unique key prefix and only delete its own.
asi.hypothesis: Split tags.spec into create+filter to reduce wall-time floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Make uploads.spec scope writes/deletes to a unique e2e-upl-<runid> prefix so it doesn't race with overview.spec.ts's e2e-upload-* seed.
asi.rollback_reason: Wall time unchanged (split tags helps tags worker but other files now bottleneck); also failing on a flake re-exposed by parallel re-balance.

description: uploads.spec cleanup: DB-level delete to avoid race with overview.spec parallel seed

asi.finding: DB-level cleanup (DELETE then assert empty) eliminates the per-item delete loop's race with overview.spec's parallel uploads seed. 0 failures, 81.9s. Test now reliable.
asi.hypothesis: Replace per-item UI delete loop with a single DB DELETE in cleanup test; eliminates cross-spec race with overview.spec's parallel upload-seed.

description: Split tags.spec into create + filter (file-level parallel rebalance)

asi.finding: Tags split into create + filter (2 files, 4 tests each). 113 passed, 0 flake. 77.4s vs 81.9s prior keep (~-5%). Each half is shorter, balancing better across workers.
asi.hypothesis: Re-apply tags split (now safe with upload race fixed). File-level parallelism rebalances workers.

description: tags-create describe parallel mode

asi.finding: Parallel mode for tags-create distributes 4 tests across workers, but with workers=2 and many other files queued, tags-create's 4 tests don't get extra parallelism — they just compete with other files for the same 2 workers. Net wall: 79.9s vs 77.4 prior.
asi.hypothesis: Mark tags-create describe as parallel mode so its 4 independent tests can run on different workers.
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise; doesn't help with workers=2.

description: Baseline: worker + L2 + L3 sequential

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for combined L2+L3+Worker bench
asi.bottleneck: L3 dominates at 81.5s (82% of total). L2=16.75s already heavily optimized from prior session. Worker=0.55s near-zero. Known l3 flake: uploads cleanup spec finds 2 upload-empty-state elements (StrictMode/double-mount).
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on L3: increase Playwright workers, fix uploads.spec strict-mode flake, or split slowest specs.

description: Fix uploads.spec strict-mode flake (3 sites use .first())

asi.finding: Fixing strict-mode violation in uploads.spec eliminates the flake. 113 passed, 0 failed.
asi.hypothesis: Use .first() on upload-empty-state assertions to handle StrictMode/dev double-mount.

description: Playwright workers=2→3

asi.finding: workers=3 cuts L3 from 78s to 61s (-21%) with 0 flakes/failures. Prior session's bad workers=3 result was confounded with earlier flake state. Confirming with second run recommended.
asi.hypothesis: With uploads.spec flake fixed and warmer dev compile, workers=3 may now be stable.

description: Confirm workers=3 stability (no code change)

asi.hypothesis: Confirm workers=3 stability

description: Playwright workers=3→4

asi.finding: workers=4 cuts L3 60s→52s with all 113 passing.
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=4

description: Playwright workers=4→5

asi.finding: workers=5: 49s, all pass.
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=5

description: Playwright workers=5→6

asi.finding: workers=6: 44s.
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=6

description: Playwright workers=6→8

asi.finding: workers=8: 43s. Diminishing returns. Bottleneck likely longest individual file (tags-create 18s; backy 14s)
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=8
asi.next_action_hint: Mark tags-create/backy as describe.parallel within file (independent tests with own slugs).

description: tags-create describe.parallel — L2 SIGTERM invalid run

asi.finding: L2 was killed early (SIGTERM) — bench result invalid for L2 portion. L3 ran fine (41.7s). Need to re-test.
asi.hypothesis: describe.parallel for tags-create's 4 independent tests
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate root cause — possibly external SIGTERM during long parallel L3 run, or PID conflict.

description: Confirm baseline after revert (workers=8)

asi.finding: Re-baseline after revert. workers=8 stable at 60s.
asi.hypothesis: Confirm post-revert baseline

description: tags-create describe.parallel (no win)

asi.finding: Within noise. tags-create's 4 tests all do beforeEach (createLink) so individual tests are still expensive. With 8 workers already saturated by 16 specs, file-internal parallelism doesn't extract more.
asi.hypothesis: describe.parallel for tags-create's 4 independent tests

description: link-crud describe.parallel

asi.finding: link-crud parallel: 42.8s same as 43.3s baseline (within noise). Tests within file already saturated by other workers. Move on.
asi.hypothesis: link-crud describe.parallel mode

description: vitest L2 isolate=false

asi.finding: Marginal improvement (60.31→59.07s, ~2%). isolate=false saves worker startup but L2 floor still bound by longest file.
asi.hypothesis: vitest pool isolate=false to skip worker fork per test file
